data.body: FINAL CONSISTENCY PASS over d251 epic + 9 children (post-rework). Verified parent-AC coverage (all 6 epic ACs map to children: AC1->S1+S2, AC2->S3, AC3->S5, AC4->S6+S6-pre, AC5->S4a+S4b, AC6->S7). FOUND + FIXED 4 cross-ticket inconsistencies introduced by the reworking: (A) ADR-number collisions — child ADRs reused 0001/0002/0003/0005/0006 which already exist in docs/adr/; renumbered to 0007-0011 (S2=0007/0008, S4b=0009, S5=0010, S6-pre=0011; S6 refers to S6-pre's by description). (B) SSM path split — S4a bot-token + S5 deploy-key were under /rebar/gerrit/* but the instance-role IAM scopes /rebar/prod/*; unified all to /rebar/prod/*. (C) S7 still said 'MCP voter' + pass3_decide {block,advisory,dropped}; aligned to S4b's PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE gate-verdict mapping. (D) S4a was orphaned (deps=[], ready=True); linked depends_on S3. Dep graph now a clean DAG: entry points S1 + S6-pre (pre-work, no live change); s2->s1; s3->s2; s4a->s3; s4b->{s3,s4a}; s5->s3; s6->{s5,s6pre}; s7->{s4b,s5,s6}. All 9 children pass structural gates (check_ac + clarity=7). MINOR OPEN: epic AC3 original wording says 'GitHub App / service account'; implementation uses a single-repo write deploy key (resolutions explain the choice) — terminological, not substantive. CAVEAT: could NOT re-run LLM plan-review to re-sign — the gate workflow is PARSE_FAILing this round ('step_budget_per_item unexpected'), consistent with the other session actively modifying the plan-review gate; epic R5 returned BLOCK(3/19) but is unreliable while the gate is mid-change. Tickets are logically consistent + structurally sound; re-sign pending the gate fix.
